data.description:
## Context
From the interactive env-var review (story 60ce). SESSION_ID is read in _commands/transition.py to tag event provenance: session = SESSION_ID (ambient, e.g. CI/agent session) or short git HEAD or "unknown". It is intentionally a GENERIC, externally-set var (NOT rebar-namespaced), so renaming to REBAR_SESSION_ID would lose the ambient pickup. Kept AS-IS for now pending a design decision.

## Open questions
- Should provenance read an ambient SESSION_ID, a rebar-owned REBAR_SESSION_ID, or both (ambient fallback)?
- Collision risk: SESSION_ID is very generic and may be set by unrelated tooling.
- Does it belong in the config surface at all, or is it pure runtime provenance?

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] EXPLICIT USER APPROVAL REQUIRED before any change to SESSION_ID handling (do not act autonomously)
- [ ] Decision recorded: keep ambient / rename / support both
- [ ] If changed: alias + docs + tests
data.title: Decide SESSION_ID env-var disposition (reads ambient external session id)
